title: Private key held as plain String in memory, no zeroize
description: persona::get_private_key returns String (not Zeroizing<String>). In delivery.rs:234 the key is stored in post_cache (HashMap<String,(Vec<u8>,String,String)>) for the full batch lifetime. In server.rs:966 a spawned tokio task holds the key as a captured String variable. Neither is zeroized on drop. A process memory dump, crash dump, or swap file exposes all private keys that have been used. Confidentiality: key material lives in heap indefinitely until GC'd by allocator.
close_reason: Won't fix: zeroize not needed for this threat model. Keys are in SQLite plaintext anyway; in-memory zeroize adds complexity without security benefit.
